page.title: Cabopino Boat Rental for Families: Calm Waters and Easy Access

data.meta_description: Discover Cabopino boat rental for family days on Costa del Sol. Calm coves, kid-friendly mangroves & easy access from Marbella. Book your family charter today.
data.summary: A guide to family-friendly boat rentals in Cabopino, Marbella, highlighting calm coves, shallow waters for swimming, and easy access from Puerto Banús. Includes fleet options, pricing, and safety features for a stress-free day on the water.
key_facts: Cabopino's sand-dune-backed coves offer gentle waves, perfect for young swimmers.
Our fleet includes 5- to 12-pax boats, all with licensed skipper and safety gear.
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) from €749/2h, all inclusive.
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) from €4,719/4h, includes a free jet ski.
All charters include fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and Spanish VAT (21%).
Puerto Banús departure means <20 min to Cabopino's protected waters.
Kid-friendly highlights: Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the Elviria sand spit.

faq.q: What is the minimum age for children on a Cabopino boat rental?
faq.a: There is no strict minimum age, but all passengers must be accounted for in the boat's capacity. Life jackets are provided for all sizes, and we recommend children under 12 wear them while underway. The skipper will brief your family on safety before departure.

faq.q: Which boat is best for a family of 5 in Cabopino?
faq.a: The Mariah SX21 or Dubhe are ideal for 5 passengers. Both are easy to board, have shaded seating, and are stable in calm Cabopino waters. For more space, the Astondoa 40 (9 pax) or Azimut 39 (11 pax) offer a flybridge, cabin, and a larger sundeck.

faq.q: Are there toilets on board family charter boats?
faq.a: Yes. All boats in our fleet except the Speedboat and Mariah SX21 have a private marine toilet (head) and a small cabin. The Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, and all Tier B boats include a full bathroom. Ask when booking if this is essential for your group.

faq.q: How long does it take to reach Cabopino from Puerto Banús?
faq.a: It takes approximately 15–20 minutes by powerboat or 30 minutes by sailing catamaran. The distance is about 5 nautical miles east along the coast. Our skippers know the direct route past the Elviria cliffs, so you spend maximum time swimming and relaxing.

faq.q: Can we anchor near the Cabopino sand dunes?
faq.a: Yes. The area in front of the Cabopino dunes (Dunas de Artola) is a protected natural park, but anchoring offshore is permitted. The water depth is 3–6 metres, with a sandy bottom. We recommend anchoring just east of the beach for the calmest conditions.

faq.q: What is included in the family charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, fresh water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden fees. You only need to bring sunscreen, towels, and swimwear.

faq.q: Is it safe for toddlers to swim in Cabopino coves?
faq.a: Yes. Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés have very gentle wave action, and the water is usually 22–26 °C in summer. The skipper will choose a sheltered spot. Life vests and buoyancy aids are available for children. Always supervise young ones near the ladder.

faq.q: How do I book a family boat in Cabopino?
faq.a: You can book directly via WhatsApp on our website. Mention 'Cabopino family day' and your preferred date. We'll confirm availability, recommend the right boat, and send you a secure payment link. Payment is 50% deposit, 50% on the day.

data.body_html:
If you are searching for a cabopino boat rental family day that combines calm waters, easy access, and genuine relaxation, you have found the right guide. Cabopino — the sandy cove just east of Marbella — is a hidden gem for parents who want their children to swim safely without the swell of open beaches. Our fleet of private charters departs from Puerto Banús, reaches Cabopino in under 20 minutes, and includes everything you need for a stress-free family outing.

Why Cabopino Works for Families

Cabopino is not a deep-sea fishing spot or a party cove. Its sheltered bay, backed by the protected Dunas de Artola, offers shallow sandy bottoms and minimal current. The two main coves — Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés — have gentle gradients, so children can wade in safely. The nearby Elviria sand spit provides a natural breakwater, keeping the water flat even when the Mediterranean breeze picks up. Water temperatures in summer average 24–26 °C, ideal for long swims.

Our Family-Friendly Fleet

We offer boats from 6.4 m to 29 m, all with licensed skippers and safety equipment. For smaller families, the Mariah SX21 (5 pax) or Dubhe (5 pax) are nimble and easy to board. For larger groups, the Astondoa 40 (9 pax) and Azimut 39 (11 pax) include a cabin, toilet, and shaded flybridge — perfect for toddlers needing a nap. The Lagoon 380 catamaran (10 pax) offers extra stability and a shallow draft, ideal for anchoring close to the beach.

Pricing for Family Charters

All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT. No surprises.

  • Tier A (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39): €749 for 2 hours → €2,299 for 8 hours.
  • Tier B (Mangusta 80 and variants): €4,719 minimum 4 hours, includes a free jet ski.
  • Other boats: pricing on request via WhatsApp — we will match the right boat to your family size.

What to Expect on the Day

You meet your skipper at Puerto Banús — we will send exact berth details. After a safety briefing (life jackets for all, including children), you cruise east past the Golden Mile and the Marbella Marina. Within 15 minutes the skyline gives way to the green pine trees of Cabopino. The skipper anchors in a calm spot, deploys the swim ladder, and you are free to paddle, snorkel, or simply float. Lunch can be eaten on board — our snacks include crisps, olives, and sandwiches, or you can bring your own picnic.

Safety Features for Young Children

Every boat carries life jackets for infants, children, and adults. The skipper is trained in first aid and will point out the location of fire extinguishers, flares, and the emergency radio. Cabopino has a lifeguard station on the main beach, and the water depth near the shore rarely exceeds 5 m. We recommend children under 12 wear a life jacket while swimming, and the skipper will help them use the ladder.

Nearby Highlights for a Family Day

Beyond the anchor, Cabopino offers a small chiringuito (beach bar) on the sand, accessible by dinghy or a short swim. The dunes are a protected natural area — you can walk the boardwalk trails after your swim. For older children, the free jet ski included with Tier B boats adds a thrill. On the return trip, you can ask the skipper to slow down past the Puerto Banús superyacht marina — always a hit with kids.

How to Book Your Cabopino Family Charter

Booking is simple. Visit our boat rental Marbella page, click the WhatsApp button, and tell us your preferred date, number of guests, and that you want a cabopino boat rental family day. We will confirm availability, recommend the best boat, and send a secure payment link. A 50% deposit secures your date; the balance is paid on arrival. No hidden fees, no fine print.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum age for children on a Cabopino boat rental?

There is no strict minimum age, but all passengers must be accounted for in the boat's capacity. Life jackets are provided for all sizes, and we recommend children under 12 wear them while underway. The skipper will brief your family on safety before departure.

Which boat is best for a family of 5 in Cabopino?

The Mariah SX21 or Dubhe are ideal for 5 passengers. Both are easy to board, have shaded seating, and are stable in calm Cabopino waters. For more space, the Astondoa 40 (9 pax) or Azimut 39 (11 pax) offer a flybridge, cabin, and a larger sundeck.

Are there toilets on board family charter boats?

Yes. All boats in our fleet except the Speedboat and Mariah SX21 have a private marine toilet (head) and a small cabin. The Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, and all Tier B boats include a full bathroom. Ask when booking if this is essential for your group.

How long does it take to reach Cabopino from Puerto Banús?

It takes approximately 15–20 minutes by powerboat or 30 minutes by sailing catamaran. The distance is about 5 nautical miles east along the coast. Our skippers know the direct route past the Elviria cliffs, so you spend maximum time swimming and relaxing.

Can we anchor near the Cabopino sand dunes?

Yes. The area in front of the Cabopino dunes (Dunas de Artola) is a protected natural park, but anchoring offshore is permitted. The water depth is 3–6 metres, with a sandy bottom. We recommend anchoring just east of the beach for the calmest conditions.

What is included in the family charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, fresh water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden fees. You only need to bring sunscreen, towels, and swimwear.

Is it safe for toddlers to swim in Cabopino coves?

Yes. Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés have very gentle wave action, and the water is usually 22–26 °C in summer. The skipper will choose a sheltered spot. Life vests and buoyancy aids are available for children. Always supervise young ones near the ladder.

How do I book a family boat in Cabopino?

You can book directly via WhatsApp on our website. Mention 'Cabopino family day' and your preferred date. We'll confirm availability, recommend the right boat, and send you a secure payment link. Payment is 50% deposit, 50% on the day.
